content.text: Answer four problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: If \(A=k[X,Y]/(X^2Y^3)\), find all minimal prime ideals of \(A\).

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Show that \(B=k[X,Y]/(X^2,Y^3)\) has a unique prime ideal, and that this prime ideal is maximal. What is it?

body.text: Which of the following polynomials are irreducible? Explain.

Suppose \(R\) is a ring with identity and \(M\) is a nonzero left \(R\)-module. Let \(\operatorname{End}_R(M)\) be the ring of \(R\)-linear maps \(M\to M\). Show that if \(0\) and \(M\) are the only \(R\)-submodules of \(M\), then \(\operatorname{End}_R(M)\) is a division ring.

subparts.label: (i)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an element of \(R\) to be irreducible, and to be prime.

subparts.label: (ii)
subparts.text: Show that a prime element is irreducible.

body.text: Suppose \(F\) is a field, \(V\) and \(W\) are \(F\)-vector spaces of finite dimension and \(f:V\to W\) is a surjective linear map, and let \(U=\operatorname{Ker}(f)\). Show using the definitions that \(\dim_F(V)=\dim_F(W)+\dim_F(U)\).

Suppose \(L/K\) is an extension of fields. Show that \(\alpha\in L\) is algebraic over \(K\) if and only if there is a nonzero sub-\(K\)-vector space \(V\subseteq L\) of finite dimension such that \(\alpha V\subseteq V\) (for “if,” pick a nonzero \(v\in V\) and consider \(\alpha^n v\) for \(n\in\mathbb{N}\)).
Find representatives of each conjugacy class of \(GL_4(\mathbb{F}_2)\) of order \(3\) or \(5\) (the order of a conjugacy class is the order of any element of it).
